title: Vriesea monstrum
taxonomy: Vriesea monstrum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae, which is a large and diverse family of tropical plants. This family includes over 3,000 species of plants, many of which are epiphytes that grow on other plants. Vriesea monstrum is a type of tillandsia, a subfamily within the Bromeliaceae family characterized by its air-purifying properties and unique, air-filled leaves. The plant's classification is further divided into genera, with Vriesea being one of the many genera within the Bromeliaceae family. As a member of the Bromeliaceae family, Vriesea monstrum is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.
uses: Vriesea monstrum, also known as the 'Vriesea Monster', is a rare and exotic plant species. Although not widely studied, Vriesea monstrum is believed to possess medicinal properties.
